LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT BRENT & FIOL'S DEMURRER TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY MARTHE SCHREIBER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRENT & FIOL, LLP'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
David Fiol Esq., of Brent & Fiol, LLP, in propria persona Defendant, (626) 240-4850.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Brent & Fiol's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by Marthe Schreiber is sustained without leave to amend. The face of the complaint and judicially noticeable documents show that both causes of action are time-barred and that there is no reasonable possibility that Schreiber would be able to amend to allege any claims that are not time-barred. No opposition filed.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)
